summary.intent: Implement fixes for 4 findings from Codex review pass 4 on the tags_input component, then sync the spec and pass the full CI gate.
summary.outcome: All 4 findings addressed (RemoveTag doc, on_tag_keydown Tab handling, SetValue exits editing, ClearAll skips ValueChange when empty), 155 tests passing, clippy clean, 97.48% line coverage, spec updated, and commit pushed.

repo: The project uses a multi-pass Codex review workflow where findings are tracked as numbered threads and resolved after implementation.
Spec lives at spec/components/selection/tags-input.md with sections §1.2 (Event enum docs), §1.6, and §1.7 that must be kept in sync with implementation.
The CI gate command is `cargo xci-fast` with 10 sub-gates; exit code 0 is the success signal.
Coverage target appears to be ~97%+ line coverage with 0 uncovered production branches.

code.finding: RemoveTag event was missing doc comments; on_tag_keydown Tab key was not handled; SetValue arm did not exit editing mode on controlled changes; ClearAll arm emitted ValueChange even when the value was already empty.

workflow: Multi-pass Codex review loop: poll verdict → fetch threads → implement → build → test → coverage check → spec sync → full gate → commit/push/resolve/re-trigger.
Spec §1.6/§1.7 blocks are regenerated from source ranges via a build script rather than hand-edited.
Tests are added in the same pass as fixes; the session went from an unspecified baseline to 155 passing tests.
Background tasks are used for long-running steps (Codex polling, xci-fast gate) with task notifications on completion.

friction: Multiple Read passes on mod.rs were needed before each Edit, suggesting the file is large enough that context placement required re-anchoring for each targeted change.
Spec §1.2 RemoveTag doc required a separate targeted edit after the §1.6/§1.7 regeneration step, indicating the two sync paths are not unified.
open_items: Commit + push + reply + resolve + re-trigger sequence was the stated next step at gate completion — verify that PR comments were actually replied to and threads resolved.
Pass 5 (and beyond) Codex review rounds may surface additional findings; the loop is designed to continue.
